title: Unit 1 — Introduction
sub: software crisis · software characteristics · software myths · legacy software · application domains

qs.t: What is software crisis? What are typical software characteristics?
What is software crisis? Briefly explain the qualities of a good software.
What is software crisis? Explain with the help of an example.
Define software. List the typical software characteristics? Justify this statement "software doesn't wear out".
What are typical software characteristics? What do you mean by software crisis? Elaborate.
What are typical software characteristics? Discuss spiral model of software development in brief.
Define software crisis. How can you say that there was software crisis in late 60s? Explain incremental model. Write its advantages and disadvantages.
What factors have contributed to the making of the present software crisis? Suggest the possible solutions to the present software crisis?
What is software crisis and what is its reason? Describe evolutionary model, in brief, explaining how it reduces crisis problem.
"Walking on water and developing software from specification are easy if both are frozen". Justify this statement.
What is software crisis? Explain with the help of example?

title: Unit 2 — The Software Process
sub: waterfall model · spiral model · prototyping · incremental model · agile · scrum · XP

h: spiral model accommodating waterfall and prototyping

qs.t: Describe Spiral model for software development. What are its advantages and disadvantages?
What is a software process model? Explain how both the waterfall model and prototyping model of software process can be accommodated in spiral process model?
What are the reasons for software runways? Explain how both the waterfall model of the software process and prototyping model can be accommodated in the spiral process model.
Explain the Prototyping model of software development. What are its advantages and disadvantages?
What do you mean by prototype? What are the risks if the prototyping becomes uncontrolled? Explain RAD in brief.
Explain details about current model of software process. Explain why the waterfall model of the software process is not an accurate reflection of software development activities.
Explain why the waterfall model of software development is not an accurate reflection of software development activities. Explain better alternative model.
Compare between waterfall model and spiral model of software development process. What is the role of user participation in selection of life cycle model?
What are the advantages and limitations of water fall process model? List out various models of software development. Explain the limitations of water fall model in detail.

Suppose a travel and tour agency needs a software for automating its book keeping activities. The set of activities to be automated are rather simple and are at present being carried out manually. The travel agency had indicated that it is unsure about the type of user interface which would be suitable for its employees and its customers. Would it be proper for a development team to use the spiral model for developing this software? Justify.

Assume that you are the technical manager of software development organization. A client approached for a software solution. The problem stated by client have uncertainties which lead to loss if not planned and solved. Which model do you suggest for his project? Justify. Explain that model with its pros and cons.
What do you mean by requirement elicitation and analysis? List the characteristics, advantages and disadvantages of Incremental Development Model.
Select and justify an appropriate software process model. [Tutorial]

title: Unit 3 — Software Requirements Engineering
sub: SRS · functional requirements · non-functional requirements · use cases · requirement elicitation · DFD · agile requirements

h: functional and non-functional requirements for given scenarios
DFD level 0 and level 1 for case studies

qs.t: What do you mean by software requirements document? Explain requirement engineering process in detail.
What is requirement engineering? Explain its steps.
Why it is so difficult to gain a clear understanding of what the customer wants? Describe the guidelines for the requirement elicitation process with suitable examples.
Why it is so difficult to gain a clear understanding of what the customer wants? What are the guidelines for the requirement elicitation process?
Give your view on requirement engineering and requirement specification.
Explain the importance of requirement engineering. List out requirement elicitation techniques. What are the problems in formation of requirements?
Briefly discuss all the activities to be carried out in problem definition and feasibility analysis.
Explain software requirement specification (SRS). What are the characteristics of a good software requirement specification document?

Prepare a list of functional requirements, level-0 and level-1 DFD for the following project: An automated ticket issuing system sells bus tickets. When the user presses the button, a menu is displayed with potential destination along with a message to the user to select a destination. Once a destination is selected, users are requested to input their credit card. Its validity is checked and the user is then requested to input their personal information. When the credit transaction has been validated, the ticket is issued.

A restaurant uses an information system that takes customer orders, sends the order to the kitchen, monitors the goods sold and inventory and generates reports for management. List functional and non-functional requirements for this Restaurant Information System.

A restaurant uses an information system that takes customer orders, sends the order to the kitchen, monitors the goods sold and inventory and generates reports for management. a) List functional and non-functional requirements for this system. b) Develop DFD level 0 and level 1 for above scenario.

Study the narration for a ride sharing system and prepare analysis and design models as specified below: [DIAGRAM NEEDED] Real-time ridesharing is a service that arranges one-time shared rides on very short notice. Vehicle owners register to the system as Service Provider and customers register as Service Seeker. The vehicle registration can be done for motorbike and car only. This type of service makes use of GPS navigation and smartphones. The system calculates estimated fare, searches nearby service providers and displays vehicle number and mobile. Once the service provider picks the seeker, system tracks the route and calculates the fare. Seeker may pay in cash or e-sewa. a) List functional and non-functional requirements for the system. b) Draw Level 0 DFD. c) Draw Level 1 DFD.

In a particular college, a sports week needs to be organized you have been assigned a role of business analyst to design a DFD diagram for the whole system. Assuming the system of business analyst to design a DFD diagram for the whole system including online registration, student council, form fill up, sport event venue and activities such as time, score card, rules and regulations, card system and prize distribution. a) Prepare the list of process and agents. b) Draw the DFD up to level 1. c) Distinguish between functional and non-functional requirements.

DFD level-0 and DFD level-1 for the case study given below: A travel agency wants an Airline Ticketing System to be developed for the office so that user can easily book flight tickets from anywhere. First of all, the customer enters the destination and data for the flight. After that, the system displays the available airlines for the same along with route or available time which is provided by the airlines company. Now the customer selects the airline which he/she finds appropriate where he/she can either book the ticket or confirm the ticket. The customer pays the ticket charge either via e-sewa or transferring the amount to the agency's bank account directly. The customer has to provide the valid email address to get the notification of booking or ticket confirmation.

A company needs to develop a Time Management System (TMS) for its executives. [Full TMS scenario describing appointment scheduling, meeting arrangement, e-mail notifications, statistics, graphical interface, remote access, leave management] a) List out all functional and non-functional requirements of the Time Management System. b) Draw a labelled DFD for the following Time Management Software (TMS). Clearly show the context diagram and its hierarchical decompositions up to level 2.

A customer presents a cheque to a clerk. The clerk checks a database containing all account numbers and makes sure whether the account number in the cheque is valid, whether adequate balance is there in the account to pay the cheque and whether the signature is authentic. Having done these the clerk gives the customer a token. The clerk also debits the customer account. If the cash cannot be paid due to an error on the cheque, the cheque is returned. The cashier calls out the token number, checks signature, pays cash, enters cash paid in daybook and files the cheque. Prepare physical and logical DFD.

In a particular school, there are various departments. There are various instructors and are having direct employment from corresponding departments. Students are admitted to school and later they choose their subject study program offered through various departments. [Full school scenario] i) Prepare the list of processes and agents ii) Draw the DFD for graduation and associated processes iii) Depict the relationship between instructor, HOD and Department

Read the case mentioned hereunder carefully: A customer visits an online movie portal. He chooses DVD movies from three different categories: Sci-Fi, Classical and Romantic and places the order for the same. He is supposed to be able to make online payment using his bank details. Upon successful transaction he is expected to receive confirmation through his e-mail. a) Make DFD level 1 for the system b) What do you mean by DFD balancing in the given case?
Draw TWO DFD diagrams for simple e-commerce site based order processing system. Assume all necessary and required specifications on your own and state them clearly first.

For better healthcare facilities in remote areas, Ministry of Health (MOH) launches Telemedicine project. Through this project expert doctor from central hospital can examine patient in remote places through video conferencing. MOH propose to maintain central server to hold all patient records and medical history. Also system should able to manage routine of doctors, appointments and follow ups. (i) list out all functional and non-functional requirement of the systems (ii) Make project Feasibility Report

Draw use case diagram for a system illustrating the interactions between a doctor, who sees patients and prescribes him medicine and treatments. List some functional and non-functional requirements in this case.
What is the purpose of use case diagrams? Construct use case diagrams and context level diagram for a library management system.

Mahanpur Nagarpalika is planning to develop new system for Tourists with all the details of their monumental structures, tourist attraction places and also cultural programs offering restaurants within the municipality. They have also plan to integrate entry tickets booking and purchasing through web as well as through app similar as the online movie ticket purchasing. i) The list of system quality attributes including both functional and non-functional requirement of the systems. ii) Complete data models with illustrative model diagram.
Explain requirement management process with necessary illustration.

title: Unit 4 — Architectural Design
sub: architectural styles · modular design · cohesion and coupling · client-server · layered architecture · multi-tier

qs.t: Differentiate between thin client and thick client model. Describe layered architecture for software.
Differentiate between thin client model and thick client model. Describe multiprocessor architecture for software.
What is software architecture? Why architecture is important to drive software development? Explain multi-tier architecture with example.
Why architecture is important to drive software development? Explain 2 tier and 3 tier architecture with example.
What do you understand by control styles in architectural design? Explain call reference architecture with suitable example.
Why is architectural design really important? What are the different types of control styles used by software engineers in designing the architectures? Explain in detail.
Describe software architecture. Explain Client-Server architecture and its importance.
What is software architecture? Why it is important in software engineering? Explain multiprocessor architecture with example.
Explain why it may be necessary to design the system architecture before specifications are written. Explain client-server architecture with appropriate example.
Why is it necessary to design the system architecture before specifications are written? Explain the different methods of modular decompositions with suitable examples.
What is software design architecture and what is its significance in software engineering? What are the common modular decomposition styles used in architectural design? Explain.
How the modular decomposition concept is practiced in system design processes? Illustrate with your own example of a second level DFD.
Compare the following: i) Client-server versus distributed object architecture
Write short notes on: a) Distributed Object architecture b) Modular decomposition

title: Unit 5 — System Modeling
sub: DFD · use case diagrams · activity diagrams · class diagrams · behavioral modeling · scenario-based analysis

h: DFD construction (level 0 and level 1) for scenarios
use case diagrams with actors and system boundary

qs.t: What is a behavior model? How does it differentiate from data model of the same system? Explain with examples and model.
What is behavior modeling in systems analysis process? Illustrate with a sample model diagram of any web-based transaction portal system.
Why system modeling is important? Mention the weakness of structured analysis method?
Compare the following: iv) Process model versus data model

title: Unit 6 — Coding and Testing
sub: verification · validation · unit testing · integration testing · black-box · white-box · TDD · V-model · inspection · code review

qs.t: Compare validation and verification. Explain software inspection process. Explain unit test, integration test and system test.
Differentiate between verification and validation. Explain how and why the V-model emphasis software V and V. Discuss various hierarchical level of testing.
Differentiate between verification and validation. Write different types of fault that can be determined from inspection. Distinguish between alpha and beta testing.
Differentiate between verification and validation. What are the types of the faults that can be uncovered by software inspection? Differentiate between black-box testing and white-box testing.
What is verification and validation? Explain their difference. Why is verification and validation planning necessary in software engineering?
Write about stub and driver testing. Differentiate between white box and black box testing.
Discuss the differences between verification and validation.
Compare and Contrast: (i) Unit testing and Integration testing (ii) Alpha testing and beta testing
Compare and contrast: (a) alpha and beta testing (b) black box and white box testing (c) unit and integration testing.
Explain software inspections and formal methods. Explain the V-model for test-based software development.
How does the SEI CMM ensure quality aspects of any complex software under development? What are the differences between ISO and CMM?

Differentiate between functional testing and structural testing. A web enabled system with a robust back-end database estimated to be of about 200 KLOC when complete. Assuming the system will work in semidetached mode, calculate the effort required per month, the development time, average number of staff required and the productivity rate. Consider COCOMO-2 for reference.
What is COCOMO? Calculate COCOMO effort, development time in calendar month, average staffing and productivity for project of application program that is estimated to be 49,200 lines of code.
What is COCOMO? Illustrate the calculation with an appropriate example.
Define regression testing. Explain cyclomatic complexity as a software metric.

Describe Cyclomatic Complexity as a software testing metrics. Use the concept of Halstead's metrics to compute the program length, program vocabulary, program volume, potential volume, program level, programming effort and time for the following code. Int x, y, z; z = 0; while (x > 0) { z = z + y; x = x - 1; } printf("%d", z);
What is verification planning? Why such planning is required? What are the different steps involved in it? Explain.
What is software verification? Clarify its role in ensuring the correctness of software implementation. Compare and contrast the Black Box and White box testing in V and V process.
In theory, formal verification could be automated if the original specification is stated completely and precisely. Why is this hard to achieve in practice? Explain.
"Validation examines the dynamic behavior of software system". Explain this with an example.
Explain why program inspection are an effective technique for discovering errors in a program? What types of error are unlikely to be discovered through inspections?

Consider a program for the determination of the nature of roots of a quadratic equation. Its input is a triple of positive integers (say a, b, c) and values may be from interval [0, 100]. The program output may have one of the following words: [Not a quadratic equation; Real roots, Imaginary roots, Equal roots]. Design test cases to test this program.
Establish the chronology among component, release unit and integration testing. Also write distinctive notes on their testing.
What is exception and error testing in the context of system implementation?
What is COCOMO? Using standard method, estimate cost of software construction process. State your assumption clearly before calculating the cost estimate.

An application has following: 10 low external inputs, 8 high external outputs, 13 logical files, 17 interface files, 11 average external inquires and complexity adjustment factor of 1.10. What are the unadjusted and adjusted function point counts?
Explain how software cost estimation is done using function point oriented and object point oriented methods.
Lines of code (LOC) and function point counts (FPC) are two measures of the size of a system. Explain advantages and disadvantages of using these two metrics for measuring systems.
Why software quality standards are needed? What are the metrics for software project size estimation? Discuss cyclomatic complexity with suitable example.
What is COCOMO? What are the different types of COCOMO models proposed? What is the problem with using lines of code?
What is the purpose of using different COCOMO models? Explain the COCOMO model in cost estimation of the software.

title: Unit 7 — Software Quality, Assurance, Maintenance
sub: SQA · FTR · CMMI levels · ISO standards · software maintenance · quality attributes · reviews and inspections

qs.t: Differentiate between ISO and CMMI standards for software quality. What are formal technical reviews? Discuss all the levels of CMMI.
What are software quality measures? Explain in details about staged CMMI model.
Explain CMMI model to evaluate the maturity of a software development.
How many levels are there in CMM? Explain in detail about all the levels.

The CMM rates software companies according to how well they identify and manage their software processes onto the 5 different levels. Explain any three out of these five levels. What advantages are there for a company to move up to the top level?
Explain how CMM encourages continuous improvement of software process. Describe various key process areas of CMM at various maturity levels.
What is software reliability and how can we measure it? Briefly explain CMM and its different levels.
Explain formal technical review process. Explain how CMMI model is used to evaluate the maturity of a software development.
Explain different levels of CMMI.
Define SQA. What are the main objectives of Formal Technical Reviews? Define term software reliability. Explain how CMM encourages continuous improvement of software process.
What are the importance of quality management in Software Development? Explain about staged CMMI Model.
What do you mean by Formal Technical Review (FTR)? How is a formal technical review conducted?
Describe software reliability and SQA.
How do you conduct formal technical review? Explain Garvin's quality dimensions.
Give a suitable definition of software quality and briefly describe the rationale for your definition. Explain with quality attributes for software.
What is Software Quality Assurance (SQA)? What steps are required to perform Statistical SQA?
What are the main objectives of Formal Technical Reviews (FTR)? What is clean room software engineering?
What are the types of software maintenance? Give some design principles for maintainability.
What are software quality measures? Why SQA is important? Explain.

h: software configuration management definition and importance

qs.t: What is software configuration management and why is it important? How could you do change management systematically in software projects?
What is the difference between version and release? Explain why we need Software Configuration Management (SCM).
What is the difference between version and variant of a system? Describe change management process in software engineering.
What are the main objectives of configuration management and version control? What is code line and baseline inversion management?
Explain the versioning process in the context of configuration management with all the associated components.
"Survival of the fittest" is valid to software industry in today's competitive market. Explain the statement in the context of issues modern software configuration management must address nowadays.
Write short notes on: Need of software configuration management
Compare the following: iii) Change management versus version management
Describe configuration management planning.

title: Unit 9 — Recent Trends
sub: DevOps · CI/CD · microservices · cloud-native · AI-assisted development · software reuse · CBSE · real-time systems

h: software reuse benefits and problems / factors for reuse planning

qs.t: What are the pros and cons of software reuse? What factors need to be taken care while software reuse planning?
What are the benefits and problems of software reuse? What factors need to be taken care of while software reuse planning?
List the different levels in which software reuse may be possible. What are the advantages and disadvantages of software reuse?
What are the main problems with software reuse? List key factors that should be considered for reusing software components.
Justify the statement "Advantages of reuse are lower costs, faster software development and lower risks." What is a design pattern?
What are the major technical and non-technical factors that hinder software reuse? Do you suggest to reuse much software and, if not, why not?
Briefly describe advantages and disadvantages of software reuse. What is COTS reuse?
What are the different factors to be considered before reusing software components. Explain.
What are the benefits and problem of software reuse? What factors need to be taken care of while software reuse planning?
Mention the situations in which the software reuse is recommended. What do you mean by design pattern?
Justify the statement "Advantages of reuse are lower costs, faster software development and lower risks."
What are the components and component-based software engineering? What are the advantages and disadvantages of using components?
What is a component? Explain the component-based software engineering (CBSE) process in brief.
What are the benefits of CBSE? What are software components? Explain with symbols for components.
What are the benefits of CBSE? How closely code generation feature of case tools are associated with CBSE? Explain.
Explain component-based software engineering (CBSE) process.
What are the reasons behind the modern tendency toward the use of Component based Software Engineering?
What is component composition? Briefly explain the use of COCOMO model.
Define real-time operating system. List out some important characteristics of RTOS.
Differentiate real-time software and other software. Explain data acquisition system.
Distinguish between a real time and non-real time system. What is a data acquisition system?
Differentiate between hard real time system and soft real time system. Outline real time system design process.
How is a real-time software different from other software? What is a data acquisition system?
How do real-time software and operating system differ from non-real time software and operating system? Describe Data Acquisition System.
Explain how is real time OS and software different from non-real time OS and software?
What specific considerations are to be made while designing typical software to be operated in real-time environment? Explain.
What is the role of data acquisition system? Explain the difference between hard and soft real time system.
Prepare a brief notes on design pattern with statement of their benefits.
